title: Question 1 (Benjamini, Kalifa and Tzalik)
statement_text: Which (if either) of the two bounds $\Omega\!\left(\frac{2^{d}}{d}\right)$ and $O\!\left(\frac{2^{d}}{\sqrt{d}}\right)$ on the minor-universality threshold of $Q_{d}$ is tight?
context_text: Benjamini, Kalifa and Tzalik (Theorem A in [5]) proved that $Q_{d}$ is $\Omega(2^{d}/d)$-minor-universal, but is not $(K\cdot 2^{d}/\sqrt{d})$-minor-universal for an absolute constant $K>0$. The upper and lower bounds differ by a factor of $\sqrt{d}$, and they asked in [5, Question 1] which bound, if either, is tight.
paper_contribution: Resolves the question by showing that $Q_{d}$ does not contain 3-uniform expander graphs with $C\cdot 2^{d}/d$ edges as minors, establishing that the lower bound $\Omega(2^{d}/d)$ is tight up to a constant factor.
notes: Reference [5] is arXiv:2501.13730 (2025); the exact wording of Question 1 is paraphrased from the introduction since the full text of [5] is not provided.
